overview.subtitle: Unlock the Hidden Patterns in Your Games

overview.body: Podable's advanced analytics transform raw game data into actionable strategic insights. Each visualization reveals different aspects of your gameplay, helping you understand not just what happened, but why it happened and how to improve.

performance.subtitle: Deep Dive Into What Makes Decks Successful

performance.body: Enhanced performance views go beyond simple win rates to analyze efficiency, consistency, and situational performance.

Combines win rate with average turn speed: Efficiency = (Win Rate × Turn Speed Factor)
Faster decision-making with good results indicates mastery of a deck.

• Standard Deviation of Game Lengths (lower = more consistent)
• Turn Pattern Reliability (do you follow similar play sequences?)
• Bracket Accuracy (does your power level match your rating?)

• Performance by Turn Order (do you play better going first/last?)
• Pod Size Impact (how do you perform in 3vs4 player games?)
• Time-of-Day Effects (are you sharper during certain hours?)

ecosystem.body: The Ecosystem Map reveals relationships between commanders in your playgroup, showing frequency pairings, head-to-head matchups, and meta health metrics.

• Node Size = How often a commander is played
• Node Color = Win rate performance
• Connections = Games played together
• Connection Thickness = Pairing frequency
This color-coded grid shows win percentages when specific commanders face each other. Darker colors indicate higher win rates in that matchup.

Diversity Score: Measures how evenly commanders are played (higher = more variety)
Balance Score: Measures win rate distribution (higher = more competitive balance)
Your meta is healthiest when both scores are above 70%. Low diversity suggests commander stagnation; low balance indicates power level mismatches.

• Identify favorable and unfavorable matchups for your commanders
• Discover underplayed commanders that might surprise opponents
• Recognize meta trends and adapt accordingly
• Find commanders that pair well together in multiplayer

gameFlow.subtitle: See the Story of Every Game

gameFlow.body: The Game Flow Heat Map displays turn-by-turn intensity for each player, revealing when games are won and lost. Each cell represents one turn, colored by its 'impact level' on the game outcome.

Turn intensity combines multiple factors:
• Turn Duration (longer turns often indicate complex, impactful plays)
• Commander Damage Dealt (aggressive turns that pressure opponents)
• Game Position (late-game turns carry more weight)
• Outcome Influence (turns that led to wins are highlighted)

🟢 Green = Low intensity (setup, passive turns)
🟡 Yellow = Medium intensity (meaningful plays)
🔴 Red = High intensity (game-changing moments)
🔵 Blue border = Your active turn
👑 Gold = Winning turn
❌ = Elimination turn

• Identify when you typically become threatening (and when others do)
• Spot patterns in successful vs failed games
• Time your interaction and threats more effectively
• Recognize when to hold back vs when to commit resources
Example: If your wins always show 2-3 consecutive red cells before victory, you know you need to 'chain together' impactful turns rather than making isolated big plays.

brackets.body: Bracket analysis helps ensure fair, fun games by tracking power level accuracy and suggesting adjustments.

Compares your bracket self-ratings with actual performance:
• Over-rating: Lower win rate than expected for bracket
• Under-rating: Higher win rate than expected for bracket
• Accurate: Performance matches bracket expectations

When opponents rate your deck differently than you do, it suggests:
• Perception Gap: Your deck feels stronger/weaker than you think
• Pilot Effect: Your skill level affects the deck's actual power
• Meta Context: Power is relative to your specific playgroup

The app analyzes your performance trends and suggests bracket adjustments:
• Consistent over-performance → Consider moving up a bracket
• Consistent under-performance → Consider moving down
• High variance → Deck power may be inconsistent

Podable's Bracket Analysis goes beyond simple self-rating. It tracks:
• Performance vs Expected (are you over/under-rating?)
• Opponent Feedback (how others perceive your deck's power)
• Dynamic Suggestions (recommended bracket adjustments based on data)

The Ecosystem Map shows bracket distribution in your meta. Healthy metas show:
• Even spread across 2-3 brackets (variety without extreme gaps)
• Low variance in win rates within brackets (balance)
• Players accurately rating their decks (self-awareness)

turns.subtitle: Master the Rhythm of Your Gameplay

turns.body: Turn analysis reveals the pacing and patterns that separate good players from great ones.

Each commander shows average turn length by turn number. This reveals:
• Setup Phase Length (how many turns until you're active)
• Decision Complexity (when your turns become most complex)
• Endgame Efficiency (do you close out games quickly?)

Successful players often show consistent turn rhythms:
• Fast early turns (quick setup decisions)
• Longer mid-game turns (complex interactions)
• Fast late-game turns (decisive action when ahead)

• If your early turns are long: streamline your setup decisions
• If your late turns are long: practice recognizing win conditions
• If patterns are erratic: work on strategic planning
